name: Vinegar and Your Dishes
recipeInstructions:
1. For stainless steel surfaces or flatware stained with stubborn streaks, lightly rub the area with olive oil. Wipe the stainless steel with a cloth dampened in vinegar. Finish by buffing to a high shine with a dry cloth.
2. To clean aluminum utensils with mineral deposits, place them in boiling water mixed with several tablespoons of vinegar. Boil for five minutes, then remove and rinse with clean water.
3. For tarnished sterling silver pieces, mix half a cup of vinegar with two tablespoons of baking soda or salt to form a thin paste. Apply the mixture to tarnished areas and work it in circular motions until all tarnish is removed. Rinse with clean water and dry thoroughly. Buff the pieces with a soft cloth for a high shine.
4. Adding a few tablespoons of vinegar to your soapy water when washing dishes reduces grease and gives the dishes a sparkly appearance.
5. Boost the potency of dishwashing liquid by adding 5 tablespoons of vinegar to the bottle, then shake well to combine.
6. Including a cup of vinegar in the rinse water prevents spotting on fine crystal or glassware and makes drying easier.
7. Adding a cup of vinegar to the final rinse cycle of your dishwasher keeps everyday glasses looking as good as new.
8. To remove heavy white film buildup on glasses, soak them in undiluted vinegar for an hour before rinsing clean with water.
